title: Delete a Project or Reference Object
breadcrumbs: Delete a Project or Reference Object
text: Delete a Project or Reference Object Deleting a project does not delete the objects it contains. It only deleted the references to those objects. To delete an object in a project, select it in the Project Structure Editor, right-click and choose Delete . Deleting a reference object does not delete the object it refers to. It remains in the Repository, where you can find and open it using the U-Bar. To delete an entire project, open it and choose Actions >Delete . You are asked to confirm that the Project should be deleted. Related Links Projects A project is a collection of references to main development objects. It is used to create and organize components, modeled entities, and libraries that are needed to build the application. Project Editor The Project Editor is automatically opened when you create or open a project in the Uniface IDE. It is used to create and organize main development objects, and export the project and its objects. Objects can belong to more than one project. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
